# User Stories — Well Production Monitoring & Allocation
**Module**: well-production  
**Generated by**: AIKit — Review Kit (@review-stories, mode: generate-stories)  
**Date**: 2026-04-25  
**Total Stories**: 48

---

## EPIC-001: Data Acquisition & SCADA Integration

---

#### US-001: Configure and Monitor PI Historian Tag Subscriptions
**Feature**: FEAT-001  **Epic**: EPIC-001  
**Story**: As a System Administrator, I want to configure which PI tags are polled per well and monitor the health of the ingestion pipeline so that I can ensure all production data reaches the system reliably.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Configure well tag subscriptions
  Given I am logged in as System Administrator
  When I navigate to Integration Settings > PI Historian
  And I select a well and add a tag name (e.g., "WHP_TBG")
  Then the tag is added to the active poll list for that well
  And the system begins collecting the tag at the next polling interval

Scenario: View pipeline health dashboard
  Given I am logged in as System Administrator or IT Operations
  When I open the Integration Health screen
  Then I see the last successful poll timestamp for each well group
  And I see a count of tags polled, tags missing, and error count in the last hour

Scenario: Change poll interval without restart
  Given the default poll interval is 1 minute
  When I update the interval for a well group to 5 minutes
  Then the system applies the new interval within 60 seconds
  And no service restart is required
```

**References**: FR-001, WF-001  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/admin/integrations/pi`, `PUT /api/v1/admin/integrations/pi/wells/{wellId}/tags`  
**UI Components**: IntegrationSettingsPanel, PITagTable  
**Database Changes**: `infrastructure.pi_tag_subscriptions`  
**Events Emitted**: `TagSubscriptionUpdated`  
**Dev Notes**: Poll interval stored in Redis config key; watcher thread picks up changes without restart.

---

#### US-002: View Real-time SCADA Readings for a Well
**Feature**: FEAT-001  **Epic**: EPIC-001  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to view the latest real-time SCADA readings for any well so that I can quickly check current operating conditions without leaving the platform.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View live readings for a producing well
  Given I am logged in as Production Engineer
  When I open the well detail page for well "ALPHA-07"
  Then I see the latest values for WHP_TBG, WHP_CSG, choke position, flowline temperature, and separator rates
  And each value shows its timestamp (e.g., "Updated 45 seconds ago")
  And values update automatically every 60 seconds on the page

Scenario: ESTIMATED flag displayed for manual wells
  Given well "PB-WEST-12" is a manual-entry Permian Basin well
  When I view its real-time readings
  Then all values are labelled with an orange "ESTIMATED" badge
  And the last entry date is shown instead of a live timestamp
```

**References**: FR-001, FR-002, BR-015  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/wells/{wellId}/readings/latest`  
**UI Components**: WellDetailPanel, LiveReadingCard, EstimatedBadge  
**Database Changes**: ADX `WellProduction.RawReadings` (read only)

---

#### US-003: Detect, View, and Resolve a Communication Loss Event
**Feature**: FEAT-003  **Epic**: EPIC-001  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to see all active Communication Loss events and approve their resolution so that affected wells are unblocked for allocation without delay.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Communication Loss event created for gap > 4 hours
  Given well "BRAVO-03" has had no data for 4 hours and 5 minutes
  When the gap detection scheduler next runs
  Then a "Communication Loss" event is created for BRAVO-03
  And the well is flagged PENDING REVIEW and locked from allocation
  And the responsible Production Engineer receives an email and in-app notification

Scenario: Production Engineer resolves a Communication Loss event
  Given a Communication Loss event exists for "BRAVO-03" with a 5-hour gap
  When I open the event and review the gap details
  And I click "Approve Resolution" and select "Accept back-fill for 4h, manual entry for 1h"
  Then the well is unlocked from allocation
  And the resolution action is logged in the audit trail

Scenario: Back-fill applied for 15-min to 4-hour gap
  Given well "CHARLIE-01" had a 2-hour data gap from 14:00 to 16:00
  When the gap detection service processes the gap
  Then readings for 14:00–16:00 are back-filled with the last-known-good value (13:59 reading)
  And each back-filled reading is flagged ESTIMATED in all downstream reports
  And no Communication Loss event is created
```

**References**: FR-003, BR-007, BR-008, WF-001  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/data-quality/gaps`, `POST /api/v1/data-quality/gaps/{eventId}/resolve`  
**UI Components**: GapEventList, GapEventDetailModal, ResolutionForm  
**Database Changes**: `data_acquisition.communication_loss_events`  
**Events Emitted**: `CommunicationLossCreated`, `CommunicationLossResolved`

---

#### US-004: Enter Manual Daily Production Readings
**Feature**: FEAT-004  **Epic**: EPIC-001  
**Story**: As a Field Operator assigned to Permian Basin West, I want to enter daily production readings for my non-SCADA wells so that the allocation engine has the data it needs to run.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Entering a valid manual production reading
  Given I am logged in as Field Operator assigned to "Permian Basin West"
  And today's readings for well "PB-WEST-04" have not yet been entered
  When I open the Manual Entry screen
  And I enter separator oil flow, gas flow, water flow, wellhead pressure, and choke size
  And I click Submit
  Then the record is saved with an ESTIMATED flag on all values
  And the entry timestamp is recorded as my submission time
  And the well no longer appears in the "Pending Entry" list for today

Scenario: Submission rejected with missing required fields
  Given I am entering readings for "PB-WEST-04"
  When I submit the form with the oil flow field blank
  Then the submission is rejected
  And a validation error highlights the missing field

Scenario: Cannot submit duplicate entry for the same well and date
  Given I have already submitted readings for "PB-WEST-04" today
  When I attempt to submit another entry for the same well and date
  Then the system rejects the submission with "Entry already exists for this well and date"
```

**References**: FR-002, BR-015, WF-002  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/wells/{wellId}/manual-readings`  
**UI Components**: ManualEntryForm, PendingWellsList  
**Database Changes**: `data_acquisition.manual_readings`

---

#### US-005: Submit LACT Tank Gauge Dip Reading
**Feature**: FEAT-004  **Epic**: EPIC-001  
**Story**: As a Field Operator, I want to submit the daily LACT tank gauge dip reading so that custody transfer volumes are reconciled against separator-based allocations.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Submit daily LACT dip reading
  Given I am logged in as Field Operator for "Gulf Coast South"
  When I navigate to LACT Entry and submit today's dip reading of 1450 BBL for lease "GCS-01"
  Then the reading is recorded with timestamp and ESTIMATED flag
  And the system calculates the variance vs. separator allocation

Scenario: LACT discrepancy alert triggered
  Given the separator-based allocation for lease "GCS-01" today is 1500 BBL
  And the LACT dip reading submitted is 1435 BBL
  When the system compares the two values
  Then a "LACT Discrepancy" alert is raised (variance = 4.3% > 1% threshold)
  And the responsible Production Engineer is notified to investigate

Scenario: Duplicate LACT entry prevented
  Given a LACT dip reading for "GCS-01" today has already been submitted
  When I attempt to submit another reading for the same lease and date
  Then the system rejects it with "LACT entry already recorded for today"
```

**References**: FR-004, clarification Q-B4  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/lact/{leaseId}/readings`  
**UI Components**: LACTEntryForm  
**Database Changes**: `data_acquisition.lact_readings`  
**Events Emitted**: `LACTDiscrepancyAlert`

---

## EPIC-002: Well Test Management

---

#### US-006: Submit a Well Test from Desktop
**Feature**: FEAT-007  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Field Operator or Production Engineer, I want to submit a well test record from my desktop browser so that the test can enter the approval workflow immediately.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Successful well test submission
  Given I am logged in as Field Operator
  When I open the Well Test Submission form for well "ALPHA-12"
  And I enter: date = today, duration = 8 hours, choke size = 32/64", oil = 450 BBL, gas = 250 MSCF, water = 80 BBL, stabilisation = confirmed
  And I click Submit
  Then the system auto-calculates BOPD = 1350, MMSCFD = 0.75, BWPD = 240, GOR = 556 SCF/BBL, WC = 15%
  And a confirmation screen shows the calculated rates for my review
  And after I confirm, the test is submitted and an L1 approval task created for the responsible Production Engineer

Scenario: Submission rejected for minimum duration
  Given I enter a test duration of 4 hours for oil producer "ALPHA-12"
  When I submit
  Then the system rejects the submission
  And displays "Minimum test duration for oil producers is 6 hours"

Scenario: Submitted record is immutable
  Given I have submitted a well test for "ALPHA-12"
  When I attempt to edit the test record
  Then the form is read-only
  And I see a message "Submitted records cannot be edited. Rejected tests require a new submission."
```

**References**: FR-010, FR-011, BR-012, WF-003  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/well-tests`, `GET /api/v1/well-tests/{testId}`  
**UI Components**: WellTestForm, RateCalculationPreview  
**Database Changes**: `well_tests.test_records`, `well_tests.approval_tasks`  
**Events Emitted**: `WellTestSubmitted`

---

#### US-007: Submit a Well Test Offline from Mobile Device
**Feature**: FEAT-008  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Field Operator working in the Permian Basin field (intermittent 4G), I want to submit a well test from my mobile device without network connectivity so that I can complete my field work and have data sync automatically when I return to signal range.  
**Story Points**: 8  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Submit well test while offline
  Given the mobile device has no network connectivity
  And I have the WellTrack Pro mobile app open (Permian Basin field operator)
  When I complete and submit a well test form for well "PB-WEST-09"
  Then the submission is stored locally in WatermelonDB
  And the pending submission appears in "Sync Queue" with status "Pending"
  And I receive a local confirmation "Saved offline — will sync when network available"

Scenario: Automatic sync when connectivity restores
  Given I have 2 pending well test submissions in the offline sync queue
  When network connectivity is restored
  Then the app automatically begins syncing within 30 seconds
  And all pending submissions are uploaded to the server
  And sync is fully complete within 60 seconds of network restore (AC-001)
  And each well test transitions from "Pending" to "Synced" in the queue view

Scenario: Sync conflict — duplicate test detected
  Given a test for "PB-WEST-09" on today's date was already submitted by another user
  When my offline submission syncs to the server
  Then the server rejects it as a duplicate
  And the app marks the item as "Sync Failed — Duplicate"
  And I receive an in-app notification with the duplicate detail
```

**References**: FR-012, AC-001, WF-003  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/sync/well-tests` (batch upload endpoint)  
**UI Components**: OfflineWellTestForm (React Native), SyncQueueScreen  
**Database Changes**: WatermelonDB local table: `pending_well_tests`; server: `well_tests.test_records`  
**Events Emitted**: `WellTestSyncCompleted`

---

#### US-008: View Sync Status of Offline Well Test Submissions
**Feature**: FEAT-008  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Field Operator using the mobile app, I want to see the sync status of all my offline submissions so that I know which tests have been received by the server and which require attention.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Sync queue shows all pending items
  Given I have 3 submissions in the offline queue
  When I open the Sync Queue screen
  Then I see all 3 items with well ID, submission time, and status
  And statuses shown are: Pending, Syncing, Synced, or Failed

Scenario: Failed sync items show resolution action
  Given one submission has status "Sync Failed — Duplicate"
  When I tap on the failed item
  Then I see the server error message
  And I have the option to "View Submitted Test" or "Discard This Draft"
```

**References**: FR-012  
**UI Components**: SyncQueueScreen, SyncStatusBadge  
**Database Changes**: WatermelonDB `pending_well_tests.sync_status`

---

#### US-009: Approve a Level 1 Well Test
**Feature**: FEAT-009  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to review and approve (or reject) submitted well tests assigned to me so that accurate test ratios are used in the allocation engine.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Approve a well test at Level 1
  Given a well test for "ALPHA-12" has been submitted and is in my L1 approval queue
  When I open the test, review the calculated rates and supporting data
  And I click "Approve"
  Then the test status changes to "L1 Approved"
  And the system checks if L2 approval is required (> 20% rate deviation)
  And if L2 is not required, the test status becomes "Approved"
  And an async job is queued to update allocation ratios within 24 hours

Scenario: L2 approval auto-triggered on > 20% rate deviation
  Given the previous approved BOPD for "ALPHA-12" was 1200
  And the new test BOPD is 1500 (25% increase)
  When I approve at L1
  Then the system routes the test to L2 approval
  And a notification is sent to all Lead Production Engineers
  And the test status shows "Awaiting L2 Approval"

Scenario: Reject a well test at Level 1
  Given I review the well test for "CHARLIE-05" and find suspicious separator reading
  When I click "Reject" and enter comment "Separator gas reading appears to be in error — please re-test"
  Then the test status becomes "Rejected"
  And the submitting operator receives a notification with the rejection comment
  And a new test submission is required (original record immutable)
```

**References**: FR-013, BR-013, WF-003  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/well-tests/{testId}/approve`, `POST /api/v1/well-tests/{testId}/reject`  
**UI Components**: ApprovalTaskList, WellTestReviewPanel  
**Events Emitted**: `WellTestL1Approved`, `WellTestRejected`, `WellTestL2Required`

---

#### US-010: Approve a Level 2 Well Test
**Feature**: FEAT-009  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Lead Production Engineer, I want to review well tests escalated for Level 2 approval so that significant rate changes are confirmed by senior engineers before affecting allocation.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Approve at Level 2
  Given a well test for "ALPHA-12" is in L2 review with a 25% rate increase
  When I review the test data and the L1 approver's notes
  And I click "L2 Approve"
  Then the test status becomes "Approved"
  And the test ratio update job is queued to run within 24 hours

Scenario: L2 approver requests re-test
  Given I review a well test that shows unexpectedly high GOR
  When I click "Request Re-test" and enter comment "GOR increase not consistent with reservoir model — please repeat test with fresh choke"
  Then the test status becomes "Rejected — Re-test Required"
  And both L1 approver and submitting operator are notified
```

**References**: FR-013, BR-013, WF-003  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/well-tests/{testId}/l2-approve`, `POST /api/v1/well-tests/{testId}/request-retest`  
**UI Components**: L2ApprovalPanel  
**Events Emitted**: `WellTestApproved`, `WellTestRetestRequested`  
**Blocks**: US-009

---

#### US-011: View Well Test History and Validity Status
**Feature**: FEAT-010  **Epic**: EPIC-002  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to view the full test history for any well including validity expiry dates so that I can plan upcoming tests before expiry blocks the monthly allocation.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View well test history
  Given I open the well detail page for "BRAVO-08"
  When I navigate to the "Well Tests" tab
  Then I see a list of all tests (newest first) with: test date, approved rates, status, and validity expiry date
  And the current active test is highlighted

Scenario: Expiry warning shown for tests expiring within 7 days
  Given the current active test for "BRAVO-08" expires in 5 days
  When I view the well tests tab or the dashboard
  Then a yellow warning badge is shown "Test expires in 5 days"
  And the well appears in the "Upcoming Test Expirations" dashboard widget

Scenario: Expired test blocks allocation finalization
  Given the current test for "BRAVO-08" expired 3 days ago
  When the monthly allocation finalization check runs
  Then "BRAVO-08" is listed as a blocker with reason "Well test expired on [date]"
  And finalization cannot proceed until the test is renewed or a VP override is logged
```

**References**: BR-016, BR-004, FR-009, FR-010  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/wells/{wellId}/well-tests`  
**UI Components**: WellTestHistoryTable, ExpiryWarningBadge  
**Database Changes**: `well_tests.test_records.validity_expires_at`

---

## EPIC-003: Production Allocation Engine

---

#### US-012: View Current Well Test Ratios for an Allocation Group
**Feature**: FEAT-011  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to see the current well test ratios for each allocation group so that I understand how volumes are being distributed before and after a new test is approved.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View allocation group ratios
  Given I navigate to the Allocation Groups screen
  When I select "Alpha Platform — Separator A04"
  Then I see each well in the group, their last approved test rates (BOPD), their ratio percentage, and the test approval date
  And the sum of all ratios equals 100.0%

Scenario: Pending ratio update shown
  Given a new test for well "ALPHA-07" was approved 3 hours ago
  When I view the allocation group
  Then "ALPHA-07" shows "Ratio update pending (< 24h)" indicator
  And the current allocation still shows the previous approved ratio
```

**References**: FR-005, FR-006, BR-009  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/allocation-groups/{groupId}/ratios`  
**UI Components**: AllocationGroupRatioTable

---

#### US-013: Select Allocation Methodology for an Allocation Point
**Feature**: FEAT-011  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to change the allocation methodology for an allocation point so that I can switch from well test ratio to back-allocation when metered data is available.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Switch to back-allocation methodology
  Given I am logged in as Production Engineer
  When I open the allocation point settings for "Alpha Separator B02"
  And I change the methodology from "Well Test Ratio" to "Back-allocation from metered streams"
  And I enter the metered stream reference ID
  And I save
  Then the allocation engine uses back-allocation for this point in the next calculation run
  And the change is logged in the audit trail

Scenario: VFM methodology is disabled (stub)
  Given I am on the allocation methodology selector
  When I hover over "Virtual Flow Metering"
  Then it appears greyed-out with tooltip "Coming in Phase 2"
  And I cannot select it
```

**References**: FR-007, clarification Q-C7  
**API Endpoints**: `PUT /api/v1/allocation-groups/{groupId}/methodology`  
**UI Components**: MethodologySelector, VFMStubTooltip

---

#### US-014: Run and View Monthly Allocation Calculation
**Feature**: FEAT-012  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to initiate the monthly production allocation calculation and view the results so that I can review volumes before sending to the Production Accountant for certification.  
**Story Points**: 8  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Monthly allocation calculated within 90 seconds
  Given it is end of month and all 340 wells have data for the period
  When I click "Run Monthly Allocation" for period "March 2026"
  Then the system calculates all allocations for all groups in parallel
  And the calculation completes within 90 seconds (NFR-002)
  And I see a summary table with well-level volumes and group reconciliation percentages

Scenario: Finalization blocked by expiry and gap events
  Given well "CHARLIE-02" has an expired test (expired 5 days ago)
  And well "BRAVO-04" has an unresolved Communication Loss event
  When I attempt to finalize the March 2026 allocation
  Then finalization is blocked with a blockers list showing both issues
  And I cannot proceed until each blocker is resolved or overridden

Scenario: Allocation vs. measured difference exceeds tolerance
  Given the allocated total for "Bravo Separator C01" is 9,200 BBL vs measured 9,249 BBL (0.53% difference)
  When finalization check runs
  Then "Bravo Separator C01" is listed as a blocker: "Allocation/measured difference 0.53% exceeds ±0.5% tolerance"
```

**References**: FR-009, BR-011, NFR-002, WF-004, AC-002  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/allocation/runs`, `GET /api/v1/allocation/runs/{runId}/results`  
**UI Components**: AllocationRunPanel, BlockerResolutionList  
**Events Emitted**: `AllocationRunCompleted`, `AllocationFinalizationBlocked`

---

#### US-015: Certify Monthly Allocation as Production Accountant
**Feature**: FEAT-012  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Accountant, I want to review and certify the monthly allocation report so that the period is locked and data can flow to regulatory reports and Snowflake.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Certify a clean allocation
  Given Production Engineer has submitted March 2026 allocation for accounting review
  When I open the certification task as Production Accountant
  And I review the allocation summary and reconciliation report
  And I click "Certify"
  Then the period status changes to "Certified and Locked"
  And no further edits are permitted without a retroactive correction request
  And the Snowflake ELT job is triggered for the finalized period

Scenario: Return for correction
  Given I find a discrepancy in well "ALPHA-05" oil volumes
  When I click "Return for Correction" and enter my comment
  Then the allocation period status returns to "In Review"
  And Production Engineer is notified with my comment
```

**References**: FR-009, WF-004  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/allocation/periods/{periodId}/certify`, `POST /api/v1/allocation/periods/{periodId}/return`  
**UI Components**: AccountantCertificationView  
**Events Emitted**: `AllocationPeriodCertified`

---

#### US-016: Submit a Retroactive Allocation Correction (≤ 30 Days)
**Feature**: FEAT-013  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to correct an allocation period within the last 30 days so that errors are fixed promptly with appropriate approval.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Submit retroactive correction for a recent period
  Given the current date is 15 April 2026
  And I want to correct well "BRAVO-09" allocation for 10 April 2026 (5 days ago)
  When I open the Retroactive Correction screen, select the period and well, enter new volumes and change reason
  And I submit
  Then an L1 correction approval task is sent to Lead Production Engineer
  And the period remains locked until approval

Scenario: Lead Production Engineer approves the correction
  Given the correction for "BRAVO-09" is in my Lead PE approval queue
  When I review and approve
  Then the allocation is recalculated for 10 April 2026
  And the change is logged with: submitter, approver, reason, old values, new values, timestamp

Scenario: Period older than 90 days rejected
  Given I attempt to open a retroactive correction for 10 January 2026 (105 days ago)
  When I submit the correction request
  Then the system rejects with "Retroactive corrections beyond 90 days require System Administrator intervention"
```

**References**: FR-008, BR-010, BR-006, WF-007  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/allocation/retroactive-corrections`  
**UI Components**: RetroactiveCorrectionForm  
**Events Emitted**: `RetroactiveCorrectionSubmitted`

---

#### US-017: Submit a Retroactive Correction Requiring Dual Approval (> 30 Days)
**Feature**: FEAT-013  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to correct an allocation period between 31 and 90 days ago so that the dual-approval requirement protects data integrity for older periods.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Dual approval triggered for period > 30 days
  Given the current date is 15 April 2026
  And I submit a correction for 10 March 2026 (36 days ago)
  When the system processes the submission
  Then both Lead Production Engineer AND Production Accountant receive approval tasks
  And the correction is applied only after both have approved

Scenario: One approver cannot override the other
  Given Lead Production Engineer has approved the correction
  But Production Accountant has not yet reviewed
  When I check the correction status
  Then it shows "Awaiting Production Accountant Approval"
  And the allocation period remains unchanged
```

**References**: BR-006, BR-010, WF-007  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/allocation/retroactive-corrections` (same endpoint, dual-approval routed by system)  
**Blocked By**: US-016

---

#### US-018: View Monthly Allocation Summary Report
**Feature**: FEAT-014  **Epic**: EPIC-003  
**Story**: As a Production Accountant or Production Engineer, I want to view the monthly allocation summary report so that I can review all well-level and field-level production volumes for a certified period.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View full monthly allocation report
  Given March 2026 allocation is certified
  When I open the Monthly Allocation Report for March 2026
  Then I see a table with each well, daily oil/gas/water volumes (gross + stock tank), and monthly totals
  And allocation group reconciliation % is shown for each group
  And ESTIMATED-flagged sources are visually distinguished

Scenario: Export to CSV
  Given I am viewing the March 2026 allocation report
  When I click "Export CSV"
  Then a CSV file is downloaded with all well-level data
  And columns include: well ID, well name, field, date, oil BBL, gas SCF, water BBL, method, estimated flag
```

**References**: FR-005, FR-009, BR-005, AC-003  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/allocation/periods/{periodId}/report`  
**UI Components**: AllocationReportTable, ExportCSVButton

---

## EPIC-004: Downtime & Loss Accounting

---

#### US-019: Log a Downtime Event
**Feature**: FEAT-015  **Epic**: EPIC-004  
**Story**: As a Field Operator, I want to log a downtime event when a well shuts in so that production loss is captured and on-stream efficiency is accurately tracked.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Log a downtime event with known end time
  Given I am logged in as Field Operator for "Alpha Platform"
  When I open the Downtime entry form for well "ALPHA-15"
  And I enter: start = 14:30, end = 18:00, category = "Mechanical failure", sub-category = "Pump failure", comment = "ESP pump tripped on overcurrent"
  And I submit
  Then the downtime event is saved
  And deferred production is automatically calculated using the current approved test rate
  And the on-stream efficiency KPI on the dashboard is updated

Scenario: Log an ongoing downtime event
  Given the well is still shut in and the end time is unknown
  When I submit the downtime form with "Ongoing" checked for end time
  Then the event is saved with status "Ongoing"
  And the well shows a red "Shut-in" indicator on the dashboard

Scenario: Unclassified category requires comment
  Given I select "Unclassified" as the downtime category
  When I try to submit with the comment field empty
  Then submission is rejected with "A comment is required for Unclassified downtime events" (BR-018)
```

**References**: FR-014, FR-015, BR-018, WF-005  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/downtime-events`  
**UI Components**: DowntimeEventForm  
**Events Emitted**: `DowntimeEventCreated`

---

#### US-020: Close an Ongoing Downtime Event
**Feature**: FEAT-015  **Epic**: EPIC-004  
**Story**: As a Field Operator, I want to close an ongoing downtime event when the well returns to production so that the final deferred production figure is accurately calculated.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Close an ongoing downtime event
  Given well "ALPHA-15" has an ongoing downtime event from 14:30 today
  When I navigate to the event and enter end time = 20:15 and click "Close Event"
  Then the event status changes from "Ongoing" to "Closed"
  And deferred production is recalculated for the full 5h 45min duration
  And the well status on the dashboard updates to "Producing"

Scenario: Cannot close event with end time before start time
  Given the downtime start was 14:30
  When I enter end time = 14:00 and try to close
  Then submission is rejected with "End time must be after start time"
```

**References**: FR-014, BR-017, WF-005  
**API Endpoints**: `PATCH /api/v1/downtime-events/{eventId}/close`  
**UI Components**: DowntimeEventPanel

---

#### US-021: View Deferred Production for Downtime Events
**Feature**: FEAT-016  **Epic**: EPIC-004  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to see the calculated deferred production for each downtime event so that I can understand the production impact of each outage.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View deferred production on event detail
  Given downtime event for "ALPHA-15" lasted 5h 45min
  And the approved test rate at time of event was 1350 BOPD
  When I open the event detail
  Then I see: Deferred Oil = 323 BBL (1350 × 5.75 / 24), Deferred Gas = calculated MMSCF equivalent
  And the formula used is shown as a tooltip (BR-017)

Scenario: Deferred production recalculated on event update
  Given the "ALPHA-15" event previously showed 5 hours duration
  When the operator closes the event with actual end time extending it to 6h 30min
  Then the deferred oil recalculates to 366 BBL
```

**References**: FR-016, BR-017  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/downtime-events/{eventId}`  
**UI Components**: DeferredProductionCard

---

#### US-022: Export Monthly Downtime Report
**Feature**: FEAT-017  **Epic**: EPIC-004  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to export the monthly downtime report in Excel and PDF format so that I can share it with leadership and archive it for operational review.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Should Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Export monthly downtime report as Excel
  Given I am viewing the downtime report for March 2026
  When I click "Export Excel"
  Then a .xlsx file is downloaded
  And it contains: one tab per platform/field, rows per well/event, subtotals per category, monthly totals per well

Scenario: Export monthly downtime report as PDF
  Given I am viewing the downtime report for March 2026
  When I click "Export PDF"
  Then a PDF is generated with formatted tables summarising by well, category, and platform/field
  And all table data is visible without truncation

Scenario: Report is filterable before export
  Given I apply a filter to show only "Mechanical failure" events for Alpha Platform
  When I export either Excel or PDF
  Then the exported file reflects the filtered data
```

**References**: FR-017  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/downtime-events/report/export?format=xlsx|pdf&period=2026-03`  
**UI Components**: DowntimeReportPage, ExportToolbar

---

## EPIC-005: Regulatory Reporting & Submission

---

#### US-023: View Auto-populated BSEE MMS-146 Draft Report
**Feature**: FEAT-018  **Epic**: EPIC-005  
**Story**: As a Regulatory Affairs Analyst, I want to view the auto-populated BSEE MMS-146 monthly report for each offshore lease so that I can verify values before submission.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: MMS-146 auto-populated after allocation finalization
  Given March 2026 allocation is certified for all offshore leases
  When I navigate to Regulatory Reports > BSEE > March 2026
  Then MMS-146 forms for all offshore leases are available
  And all production volume fields are pre-filled from the certified allocation data
  And each field shows a source reference (well ID, allocation group)

Scenario: Discrepancy identified and returned for correction
  Given I review the MMS-146 for lease "OCS-G-1234" and find well "BRAVO-07" volumes appear low
  When I click "Return for Allocation Correction"
  Then I am guided to the retroactive correction workflow (WF-007)
  And the MMS-146 for March 2026 status shows "Awaiting Allocation Correction"

Scenario: Comparison with prior month shown
  Given I am reviewing the March 2026 MMS-146
  Then a side-by-side comparison with February 2026 submitted values is displayed
  And fields with > 20% variance are highlighted for review
```

**References**: FR-018, WF-006  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/regulatory/bsee/reports/{period}`  
**UI Components**: MMS146ReportViewer, AllocationSourceTooltip

---

#### US-024: Submit BSEE MMS-146 Report
**Feature**: FEAT-020  **Epic**: EPIC-005  
**Story**: As a Regulatory Affairs Analyst, I want to submit the approved MMS-146 report via SFTP to BSEE eFAST so that the regulatory obligation is fulfilled and the report is locked.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Successful BSEE SFTP submission
  Given the March 2026 MMS-146 for lease "OCS-G-1234" has been reviewed and approved
  When I click "Submit to BSEE eFAST"
  Then the system generates the CSV file in MMS-146 April 2025 fixed-column format
  And uploads the file to the eFAST SFTP drop folder
  And records: submission timestamp, SFTP transfer reference, and report period
  And the report status changes to "Submitted — Locked"
  And the report becomes read-only

Scenario: SFTP submission failure handled
  Given the SFTP server is temporarily unreachable
  When submission is attempted
  Then the system retries 3 times with 5-minute intervals
  And after 3 failures, I am notified by email and in-app alert
  And the report status shows "Submission Failed — Retry Available"

Scenario: Amendment after submission
  Given the March 2026 MMS-146 for "OCS-G-1234" is in "Submitted — Locked" status
  When I click "Create Amendment"
  Then a new amendment record v2 is created with the original data pre-filled
  And I must enter a justification before the amendment can be submitted
  And the original submission record is preserved (immutable)
```

**References**: FR-021, BR-014, WF-006, clarification Q-D2  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/regulatory/bsee/reports/{reportId}/submit`  
**Events Emitted**: `BSSEReportSubmitted`, `BSSESubmissionFailed`

---

#### US-025: View and Submit Texas RRC PR-2 Report
**Feature**: FEAT-019  **Epic**: EPIC-005  
**Story**: As a Regulatory Affairs Analyst, I want to view the auto-populated Texas RRC PR-2 report and submit it via the RRC REST API so that onshore reporting obligations are met.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: PR-2 auto-populated for Texas onshore wells
  Given March 2026 allocation is certified for Permian Basin West and Gulf Coast South
  When I navigate to Regulatory Reports > Texas RRC > March 2026
  Then the PR-2 report is pre-populated for all Texas onshore wells
  And data maps correctly to all RRC v3.2 schema fields

Scenario: Submit PR-2 via RRC REST API
  Given the March 2026 PR-2 has been reviewed and is ready
  When I click "Submit to Texas RRC"
  Then the system obtains an OAuth2 token using client credentials from Key Vault
  And submits the report to the RRC v3.2 REST API endpoint
  And records the RRC confirmation reference number
  And the report status changes to "Submitted — Locked"

Scenario: Failed submission retried automatically
  Given the RRC API returns a 503 on first attempt
  When the system retries
  Then it retries up to 3 times with exponential backoff
  And alerts the Regulatory Analyst if all retries fail
```

**References**: FR-020, clarification Q-D3  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/regulatory/rrc/reports/{reportId}/submit`  
**Events Emitted**: `RRCReportSubmitted`

---

#### US-026: Receive Automated BSEE Deadline Reminders
**Feature**: FEAT-021  **Epic**: EPIC-005  
**Story**: As a Regulatory Affairs Analyst and VP Operations, I want to automatically receive email reminders before the BSEE submission deadline so that we never miss the 15th-of-month cutoff.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: 10-day reminder sent when report not yet submitted
  Given today is 5 April 2026 (10 days before the 15 April BSEE deadline)
  And the March 2026 BSEE report is in "Draft" status
  When the daily reminder scheduler runs at 08:00 UTC
  Then emails are sent to Regulatory Affairs Analyst and VP Operations
  And the email body states: period, deadline date (15 April), and a deep link to the report

Scenario: 3-day reminder sent
  Given today is 12 April 2026
  And the March 2026 BSEE report is still not submitted
  When the scheduler runs
  Then a second reminder email is sent with "3 days remaining" subject line

Scenario: No reminder sent if already submitted
  Given the March 2026 BSEE report was submitted on 8 April 2026
  When the 12 April scheduler runs
  Then no reminder email is sent
```

**References**: FR-019, BR-019  
**API Endpoints**: N/A (background scheduler job)  
**Events Emitted**: `RegulatoryReminderSent`

---

## EPIC-006: Production Dashboards & Analytics

---

#### US-027: View Real-time Production KPI Dashboard
**Feature**: FEAT-022  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to view all critical production KPIs on a single dashboard page so that I immediately understand the current state of the field when I start my shift.  
**Story Points**: 8  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Dashboard loads with all KPIs within 10 seconds
  Given I am logged in as Production Engineer
  When I navigate to the Production Dashboard
  Then within 10 seconds (NFR-001) I see:
    - Total field production: current 24h BOPD, MMSCFD, BWPD
    - On-stream efficiency: % per platform and total fleet
    - Top 5 wells by production rate (BOPD)
    - Top 5 wells by deferred production (month-to-date, BBL)
    - Well count tiles: Producing / Shut-in / Injection / Workover

Scenario: Filter dashboard by platform
  Given the dashboard is showing all-field data
  When I select "Alpha Platform" from the field filter
  Then all KPI tiles update to show Alpha Platform data only
  And the URL updates to reflect the filter (shareable link)

Scenario: "Top 5 Deferred" toggle to rolling 30-day view
  Given I am on the dashboard
  When I click the "Rolling 30 days" toggle on the Deferred Production widget
  Then the top 5 deferred wells refresh using rolling 30-day window instead of month-to-date
```

**References**: FR-022, NFR-001, BO-004  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/dashboard/kpis?field=all|alpha|bravo|charlie|permian|gulfcoast`  
**UI Components**: KPIDashboard, ProductionTile, OSETile, TopWellsTable, WellStatusTiles  
**Database Changes**: Redis cache key: `dashboard:kpis:{field}` (TTL 60s)

---

#### US-028: Filter Dashboard by Platform or Field
**Feature**: FEAT-022  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Field Operator or Production Engineer, I want to filter the dashboard to show only my assigned field so that I focus on relevant assets immediately.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Field Operator auto-filtered to their field
  Given I am a Field Operator assigned to "Permian Basin West"
  When I log in and land on the dashboard
  Then the dashboard automatically filters to "Permian Basin West" data
  And I cannot navigate to other fields (asset-scope restriction)

Scenario: Production Engineer can switch fields freely
  Given I am a Production Engineer with access to all fields
  When I change the field filter to "Charlie Platform"
  Then all KPIs update within 5 seconds
```

**References**: FR-022, clarification Q-C4, NFR-005  
**UI Components**: FieldFilterDropdown  

---

#### US-029: Receive and Acknowledge an Over-rate Production Alert
**Feature**: FEAT-023  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to receive an alert when a well is producing significantly above its test rate for multiple days so that I can investigate and take appropriate action.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Over-rate alert generated
  Given well "ALPHA-07" has been producing at 1750 BOPD for 3 consecutive days
  And its approved test rate is 1350 BOPD (29.6% above — exceeds 15% threshold, BR-001)
  When the anomaly detection service evaluates the third consecutive day
  Then an over-rate alert is created for "ALPHA-07"
  And the responsible Production Engineer receives an email and in-app notification

Scenario: Alert acknowledged and resolved
  Given the over-rate alert for "ALPHA-07" is in my alert feed
  When I investigate and determine the choke was changed since the last test
  And I click "Acknowledge" and add note "New choke size installed — scheduling new well test"
  And I submit a new well test (US-006)
  Then I can mark the alert as "Resolved" after the new test is approved
  And the resolution note is stored in the audit trail
```

**References**: BR-001, WF-008  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/alerts`, `PATCH /api/v1/alerts/{alertId}/acknowledge`  
**UI Components**: AlertFeed, AlertDetailModal  
**Events Emitted**: `OverRateAlertCreated`

---

#### US-030: Handle a High Water Cut Alert
**Feature**: FEAT-023  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Reservoir Engineer, I want to receive a High Water Cut alert when any oil producer exceeds 95% water cut so that I can assess the well's producing life and recommend intervention.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: High Water Cut event created
  Given well "CHARLIE-11" has reported WC = 96.2% in the latest test
  When the anomaly detection service processes the test
  Then a "High Water Cut" event is created for "CHARLIE-11"
  And the Reservoir Engineer is notified via email and in-app

Scenario: Reservoir Engineer responds to the alert
  Given the High Water Cut event for "CHARLIE-11" is in my alert queue
  When I open the event and review the WC trend chart
  And I add my assessment note and click "Close with Note"
  Then the event is marked resolved with my note
  And the note is visible to Production Engineers on the well detail page
```

**References**: BR-002, WF-008  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/alerts/{alertId}/resolve`  
**Events Emitted**: `HighWaterCutEventCreated`

---

#### US-031: Handle a GOR Breach Review Workflow
**Feature**: FEAT-023  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Reservoir Engineer, I want a formal review workflow created when a well's GOR exceeds 125% of the approved reservoir GOR so that the anomaly cannot be dismissed without my documented assessment.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: High  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: GOR breach review workflow created
  Given well "BRAVO-06" has GOR = 1875 SCF/BBL and approved reservoir GOR = 1400 SCF/BBL (34% above — exceeds 25% threshold, BR-003)
  When the anomaly detection service evaluates the test data
  Then a GOR Breach review workflow is created and assigned to the Reservoir Engineer

Scenario: Workflow cannot be closed without Reservoir Engineer note
  Given the GOR Breach workflow for "BRAVO-06" is open
  When I attempt to close the workflow without entering a note
  Then the close action is rejected with "A Reservoir Engineer note is required to close this review (BR-003)"

Scenario: Workflow closed with Reservoir Engineer note
  Given I enter my assessment: "GAS cap breakthrough confirmed — recommend ESP speed reduction and accelerated workover evaluation"
  When I click Close
  Then the workflow is closed with my note
  And the note is linked to the well record and audit trail
```

**References**: BR-003, WF-008  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/alerts/{alertId}/close`  
**Events Emitted**: `GORBreachReviewClosed`

---

#### US-032: View Well Decline Curve
**Feature**: FEAT-024  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Reservoir Engineer, I want to view a well's production history overlaid with its approved type curve so that I can assess whether production is tracking as expected.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Should Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: View decline curve for a well
  Given well "ALPHA-09" has a type curve uploaded and 18 months of production history
  When I navigate to the well detail page and open the "Decline Curve" tab
  Then I see a chart with daily production rate (BOPD) on Y-axis, date on X-axis
  And the historical production line is shown in blue
  And the type curve is overlaid in orange dashed line
  And the chart covers at least 24 months (padded with type curve for future)

Scenario: Toggle between oil and gas rate view
  Given the decline curve tab is open for "ALPHA-09"
  When I click "Gas Rate (MMSCFD)"
  Then the chart updates to show gas production history alongside the type curve
  And ESTIMATED-flagged data points are shown as hollow dots (visual distinction)
```

**References**: FR-024, clarification Q-C6  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/wells/{wellId}/decline-curve`  
**UI Components**: DeclineCurveChart (ECharts)

---

#### US-033: Export Dashboard Chart and Data
**Feature**: FEAT-025  **Epic**: EPIC-006  
**Story**: As a Production Engineer, I want to export any dashboard chart as a PNG and its underlying data as a CSV so that I can include production data in reports and presentations.  
**Story Points**: 2  
**Priority**: Should Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Export chart as PNG
  Given I am viewing the "Total Field Production" KPI time-series chart
  When I click the chart's "⋮" menu and select "Export PNG"
  Then a PNG image of the chart is downloaded

Scenario: Export underlying data as CSV
  Given I click "Export CSV" on the same chart
  Then a CSV file is downloaded with columns: date, well/field, value, unit, estimated_flag
  And the CSV covers the same date range currently displayed in the chart
```

**References**: FR-023  
**UI Components**: ChartExportMenu

---

## EPIC-007: Platform Foundation

---

#### US-034: Authenticate via Azure AD SSO
**Feature**: FEAT-026  **Epic**: EPIC-007  
**Story**: As any user, I want to log in to WellTrack Pro using my company Azure AD credentials so that I don't need a separate password.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Successful login via Azure AD SAML 2.0
  Given I navigate to the WellTrack Pro URL
  When I am redirected to the company Azure AD login page
  And I authenticate with my corporate credentials
  Then I am redirected back to WPMA and logged in
  And my role permissions are loaded from my Azure AD group memberships
  And no WPMA-local password was used

Scenario: Unauthenticated access redirected to login
  Given I am not logged in
  When I attempt to access any WPMA page directly by URL
  Then I am redirected to Azure AD login

Scenario: Session expiry handled gracefully
  Given my session token has expired while on a dashboard page
  When I attempt an API call
  Then I receive a transparent re-authentication redirect to Azure AD
  And I return to the same page after re-authentication
```

**References**: NFR-004, clarification Q-E3  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /auth/login`, `GET /auth/callback`  
**UI Components**: AuthGuard (React HOC)

---

#### US-035: Assign and Manage User RBAC Roles and Asset Scopes
**Feature**: FEAT-026  **Epic**: EPIC-007  
**Story**: As a System Administrator, I want to assign RBAC roles and asset scopes to users so that each person has appropriate access to the data and functions relevant to their job.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Assign a role to a user
  Given I am logged in as System Administrator
  When I search for user "jane.smith@meridianenergy.com" in User Management
  And I add role "Production Engineer" and asset scope "Alpha Platform, Charlie Platform"
  And I save
  Then Jane can log in and see wells for Alpha and Charlie platforms only
  And she cannot see Permian Basin or Gulf Coast wells

Scenario: System Administrator role is exclusive
  Given I try to assign "System Administrator" role to a user who already has "Production Engineer"
  When I save
  Then the system warns "System Administrator role must be assigned exclusively"
  And requires me to remove other roles before assigning SysAdmin

Scenario: User with multiple roles gets additive permissions
  Given user "bob.jones" has both "Production Engineer" and "Regulatory Analyst" roles
  When Bob logs in
  Then he has combined permissions from both roles
```

**References**: NFR-005, clarification Q-E1  
**API Endpoints**: `PUT /api/v1/admin/users/{userId}/roles`  
**UI Components**: UserManagementTable, RoleAssignmentModal

---

#### US-036: Query the Audit Log
**Feature**: FEAT-027  **Epic**: EPIC-007  
**Story**: As a System Administrator or Production Accountant, I want to search the audit log by entity, user, and date range so that I can investigate any data change for compliance or dispute resolution.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Must Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Search audit log by well and date range
  Given I am logged in as System Administrator
  When I navigate to Audit Log and filter: entity = "allocation" and well = "ALPHA-07" and date range = March 2026
  Then I see all allocation changes for ALPHA-07 in March 2026
  And each record shows: user, action, old value, new value, and ISO-8601 timestamp

Scenario: Audit records are read-only
  Given I am viewing an audit log record
  When I attempt to edit or delete the record via UI or direct API call
  Then the API returns HTTP 403 "Audit records are immutable"

Scenario: 100% of production changes captured
  Given a well test was approved and allocation ratios updated during the acceptance test
  When I query the audit log for that well and time window
  Then I can find all three change records: test approval, ratio update, and allocation recalculation
```

**References**: NFR-007, AC-005  
**API Endpoints**: `GET /api/v1/audit-log?entity=&entityId=&userId=&from=&to=`  
**UI Components**: AuditLogSearchPage  
**Database Changes**: `foundation.audit_log` (read only via this endpoint)

---

#### US-037: Manage Well Master Data
**Feature**: FEAT-028  **Epic**: EPIC-007  
**Story**: As a System Administrator, I want to create and maintain well master records so that the system always has accurate well metadata driving field filtering, allocation grouping, and regulatory mapping.  
**Story Points**: 5  
**Priority**: Critical  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Create a new well
  Given I am logged in as System Administrator
  When I navigate to Well Administration and click "New Well"
  And I enter: well ID, name, well type (oil/gas), platform/field, allocation group, commodity, shrinkage factor
  And I save
  Then the well appears in all lists and is available for tag subscription and data entry

Scenario: Deactivate a well
  Given well "ALPHA-03" has been permanently abandoned
  When I set well status to "Deactivated" and provide a deactivation date and reason
  Then the well no longer appears in active well lists for operators
  And all historical data for the well is preserved and queryable
  And any active allocation group assignment for the well is removed

Scenario: Configure allocation point (group → wells mapping)
  Given I need to add new well "ALPHA-23" to "Alpha Separator A04" allocation group
  When I edit the allocation group and add ALPHA-23 with its initial test rate
  Then the next allocation run includes ALPHA-23 in the group calculation
```

**References**: NFR-005, FR-001, clarification Q-B3  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/admin/wells`, `PUT /api/v1/admin/wells/{wellId}`, `PUT /api/v1/admin/allocation-groups/{groupId}/wells`  
**UI Components**: WellAdminTable, WellForm, AllocationGroupEditor

---

#### US-038: Import Well Type Curves
**Feature**: FEAT-028  **Epic**: EPIC-007  
**Story**: As a Reservoir Engineer, I want to upload a type curve CSV for a well so that the decline curve visualization has the correct expected production profile to compare against actuals.  
**Story Points**: 3  
**Priority**: Should Have  

**Acceptance Criteria**:
```gherkin
Scenario: Upload a valid type curve CSV
  Given I am logged in as Reservoir Engineer
  When I navigate to Well Detail > Decline Curve > "Upload Type Curve"
  And I upload a CSV with columns: date, expected_bopd, expected_mmscfd
  Then the system validates the format and stores the type curve
  And the decline curve chart immediately shows the new type curve

Scenario: Invalid CSV format rejected
  Given I upload a CSV with missing "expected_bopd" column
  When I submit
  Then the upload is rejected with "Missing required column: expected_bopd"
  And the existing type curve (if any) is unchanged
```

**References**: FR-024, clarification Q-C6  
**API Endpoints**: `POST /api/v1/admin/wells/{wellId}/type-curve`  
**UI Components**: TypeCurveUploadModal

---
